items.title: Consistent tagging conventions
items.avoid: Free-form tags like `good`, `try2`, `final-final` with no team glossary.
items.prefer: Agreed prefixes: `candidate`, `baseline`, `data-v2`, `failed-oom` documented in team README.
items.why: Predictable tags make filters and automated comparison reliable across projects.
items.example: Tag every production-ready run `candidate` and the champion `candidate-champion` before registry promotion.

items.title: Log lineage fields on every run
items.avoid: Logging only accuracy while omitting git commit, data hash, and seed.
items.prefer: Log `git_sha`, `data_version`, and `seed` as params or tags on the same run as metrics.
items.why: Run comparison and registry audits require context beyond a single score.

items.title: Compare before register
items.avoid: Registering the first run that beats a manual threshold without side-by-side review.
items.prefer: Filter tagged candidates, compare params and metrics, then register one run ID with documented rationale.
items.why: Registry versions are costly to roll back; experiment storage is cheap for exploration.
items.example: Compare three `candidate` run IDs in the UI, pick `def456` for highest val_f1 at same latency, then register.

items.title: Wire deployment to registry metadata
items.avoid: Deploying artifact paths copied from a laptop folder.
items.prefer: Load Production stage model by registry name/version and record source run ID in service config.
items.why: Production incidents need instant traceability to training configuration.
items.example: Deploy `models:/fraud-detector/Production` and store `source_run_id=7b2e10` in the release manifest.
